name: Glassmorphism
description: Frosted glass effect with blur and transparency. Creates depth and modern feel.
implementation: background: rgba(255 255 255 / 0.1); backdrop-filter: blur(16px); border: 1px solid rgba(255 255 255 / 0.2); border-radius: 16px;
use_cases: SaaS dashboards fintech hero cards dark mode UIs
do_examples: Use on dark or gradient backgrounds where blur shows. Add subtle border for definition.
dont_examples: Use on white backgrounds — blur has no effect. Avoid on performance-critical mobile views.

name: Neumorphism
description: Soft extruded UI that looks pushed or pressed into the background surface.
use_cases: Calculator apps settings panels wellness apps
do_examples: Keep background and element colors identical. Works only on light mid-tone backgrounds.
dont_examples: Never use on dark backgrounds. Avoid for text-heavy interfaces — poor accessibility.

name: Flat Design
description: Clean minimal design with no shadows depth or gradients. Pure color and shape.
implementation: No box-shadow. Solid fills only. 2px border for definition. Clean icons. Generous whitespace.
use_cases: Marketing sites product landing pages mobile apps
do_examples: Use bold typography and strong color contrast to add hierarchy without depth.
dont_examples: Don't remove all visual cues — flat can become invisible. Add hover states for interactivity.

name: Brutalism
description: Raw intentional ugly-beautiful aesthetic with stark contrast harsh fonts visible structure.
implementation: font-family: monospace or Impact. border: 3px solid black. No border-radius. High contrast colors.
use_cases: Creative portfolios art fashion controversial brands
do_examples: Commit fully — half-brutalism looks like a mistake. Use for brand differentiation.
dont_examples: Never use in healthcare finance or any trust-sensitive industry.

name: Skeuomorphism
description: Design that mimics real-world objects textures and materials.
implementation: background-image: url('texture.png'); box-shadow: inset 0 1px 3px rgba(0 0 0 0.3); border-radius: 4px;
use_cases: Game UIs audio apps nostalgia products leather/wood brands
do_examples: Use sparingly for specific interactive components like toggles or dials.
dont_examples: Avoid full skeuomorphic layouts in 2024 — looks dated outside intentional retro contexts.

name: Minimalism
description: Maximum whitespace minimum elements. Every element earns its place.
implementation: padding: 80px 5%. Single font family. 60/30/10 color rule. No decorative elements.
use_cases: Luxury brands portfolios editorial journalism premium SaaS
do_examples: Remove until it breaks then add one thing back. Use one bold typographic choice.
dont_examples: Don't confuse minimal with empty — content still needs clear hierarchy.

name: Dark Mode
description: Dark backgrounds that reduce eye strain and create premium feel.
use_cases: Developer tools gaming fintech crypto dashboards night apps
do_examples: Use true dark (#0F172A not #000000). Add subtle surface elevation via lighter backgrounds.
dont_examples: Don't just invert a light design. Avoid pure black — causes halation on OLED screens.

name: Micro-interactions
description: Small delightful animations that respond to user actions and provide feedback.
implementation: transition: all 0.2s ease; transform: scale(1.02) on hover. Loading spinners. Success checkmarks.
use_cases: Any product that wants to feel polished and thoughtful
do_examples: Keep under 300ms for hover. Under 500ms for page transitions. Add easing curves.
dont_examples: Never animate on scroll if it causes content to jump or shift layout unexpectedly.

name: Card-based Layout
description: Content organized in contained rectangular units with consistent spacing.
implementation: display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill minmax(280px 1fr)); gap: 24px;
use_cases: Dashboards e-commerce news feeds social platforms
do_examples: Add hover state. Include clear hierarchy: image → headline → meta → CTA.
dont_examples: Avoid cards without visual hierarchy — every card looking the same creates decision paralysis.

name: Hero + Centered CTA
description: Full-width hero with centered headline and single primary CTA above fold.
implementation: height: 100vh or min-height: 600px. Headline + subtext + 1 primary + 1 secondary CTA. Background image or gradient.
use_cases: Landing pages SaaS homepages product launches
do_examples: One primary CTA only. Write headline as outcome not feature. Add social proof below hero.
dont_examples: Never put form fields in hero for cold traffic. Avoid stock hero imagery.

name: Asymmetric Grid
description: Off-grid intentional layouts that break from symmetry to create visual tension and interest.
implementation: CSS Grid with named areas. Mix 4-col and 8-col within same page. Intentional overlap.
use_cases: Creative agencies portfolios fashion luxury editorial
do_examples: Works best with strong typography to anchor the asymmetry.
dont_examples: Looks broken without design intent. Don't use for data-heavy UIs where scanability matters.

name: Bento Grid
description: Mosaic of varied card sizes inspired by Mac marketing. Creates visual interest from mixed content.
implementation: display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(4 1fr); Mixed span-2 span-3 cards. Consistent gap.
use_cases: Product feature sections marketing pages app showcases
do_examples: Mix card sizes intentionally — vary 1x1 1x2 2x1 2x2. Keep gap consistent (16-24px).
dont_examples: Avoid odd numbers of items — asymmetry needs to feel intentional not accidental.
